system.description: Traps and filters are implanted at the top of the trachea just below the larynx (voice box). This cluster of specialized tissue absorbs airborne impurities and keeps them from reaching and subsequently affecting the lungs. Solid matter (smoke, pollen, dust, and so on) is easily blocked and expelled with outgoing breath. Gaseous compounds are rendered inert or less effective through the various chemical reactions performed by the filters. Though breathing becomes difficult, the subject will suffer fewer ill-effects (unless the gas itself is inert). Impurities of bacterial size and smaller are not affected by tracheal filters. Each level of filter acts to resist non-microbiological air-vectored attacks. Reduce the Power Level of such an attack by one-half the level of the filter (round down) of the character making the Resistance Test. That is, against a SS2 gaseous stock, an individual with a Level 4 tracheal filter resists against a 352 Damage Code.
